title: Message Envelopes, Bytes, and Schema Evolution · Goroutine Forensics
summary: An incident packet contains a plausible a bounded Go envelope decoder and compatibility matrix with one stale generation, blocked notification, goroutine leak, acknowledgement, route, or effect defect; locate cause before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a bounded Go envelope decoder and compatibility matrix and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, a…
objectives: Bound message bytes, verify content type and encoding, decode once, parse safely, and keep malformed content out of domain effects; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Define message ID, type, schema version, source, subject, occurrence time, correlation, causation, tenant, and trace context without conflating them; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Validate unknown envelopes and payloads after bounded decoding with required fields, semantic checks, collection limits, and explicit rejection evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Buffer Decoding Boundary
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Go messaging team handles case 5802 during table-driven topology review for a tenant migration. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound bytes and validate content metadata, identity, and version before decoding effects; prove cleanup under a single deadline and cancellation cause; then add a subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Bound message bytes, verify content type and encoding, decode once, parse safely, and keep malformed content out of domain effects; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Inspect the Buffer Decoding Boundary trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Buffer Decoding Boundary.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Asyncapi Cloudevents Contract
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: rabbit-go-asyncapi-cloudevents-contract-7otx64" that uses case 6189 to bound bytes and validate content metadata, identity, and version before decoding effects. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import amqp091-go, open sockets, contact RabbitMQ, Docker, networks, databases, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5 compile, vet, race, fuzz, leak, amqp091-go 1.12.0, RabbitMQ 4.3, TLS, partition, restart, load, upgrade, and production gates.
